Reports

Merlin includes three built-in reports accessible from the Reports section of the navigation. All reports operate on posted purchase invoices and default to the current financial year as their date range. Navigating to /reports redirects automatically to the first report.

Expenses by Account

Found at /reports/expenses-by-account. This report shows your total posted purchase invoice spend grouped by GL account over the selected date range. It answers the question: "How much did we spend on each expense category?" Use this report to understand cost allocation and prepare for budget reviews. NEEDS CONFIRMATION: exact grouping and filters available on this page (e.g. whether sub-accounts are rolled into parent accounts).

Expenses by Supplier

Found at /reports/expenses-by-supplier. This report shows total posted purchase invoice spend grouped by supplier over the selected date range. It answers the question: "How much did we spend with each vendor?" Use it to identify your largest suppliers and review spending concentration. NEEDS CONFIRMATION: exact grouping and filters available on this page.

LLM Performance

Found at /reports/llm-performance. This report provides aggregated metrics from the llm_logs table over the selected date range. Metrics include token usage totals, confidence score distribution, which model tier (fast/strong/backup) handled each invoice, and how frequently the strong-model fallback was triggered. Use this report to understand AI extraction quality over time and to estimate API usage costs. Viewing this report requires the view-llm-summary permission in addition to the base document permissions. For individual extraction details, see LLM Logs.

Date ranges and the financial year

All three reports default to the current financial year as their date range. The financial year boundaries are calculated by FinancialYearService using the financial_year_start_month setting from Settings › General. For example, if the financial year starts in March, the current financial year runs from 1 March to the last day of February the following year. You can change the date range on each report to any custom period. NEEDS CONFIRMATION: whether the default date range varies by report.

Permissions

NEEDS CONFIRMATION: the exact permissions required to access each report page. The LLM Performance report additionally requires view-llm-summary. The expense reports likely require documents-view-any.
